---
title: "LRP98: Negative-control outcome — letter sounds (LS) -> expressive vocabulary (EV)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

Tier-1 decoding-specificity mini-suite, design 1B (see
`notes/202607172330-tier1-decoding-specificity-spec.md`). A **negative-control outcome**
for the letter-sound slope (Lipsitch, Tchetgen Tchetgen & Cohen 2010,
[doi:10.1097/EDE.0b013e3181d61eeb]).

Under the revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`) letter-sound knowledge $LS$ has
descendants only $\{NW, PA, PS, WR\}$, so expressive vocabulary $EV$ is **not** a causal
descendant of $LS$ (no $LS \to \cdots \to EV$ path). Any adjusted $L \to E$ association is
purely backdoor through the shared parents $\{A, GA, HS, IG, IS, SP\}$ — the same source
of confounding as the reading outcomes.

**Read.** The $L$ slope should be $\approx 0$ here; a clearly-positive slope would flag
that letter sounds are proxying general ability / teaching dose rather than acting on the
written code.

## Adjustment set

$\{G, A, HS, IS, SP\}$ plus baseline $E_\text{pre}$ — identical to the rest of the Tier-1
panel. GA unblockable; adjusted association only.

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
